Class BuildingState

java.lang.Object
Controller.State
Controller.RealTimeBuilding.BuildingState
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class BuildingState extends State
State that manages the real-time ship building phase. During this phase, players simultaneously assemble their ships by drawing components from a common pool and placing them on their boards. The class coordinates: - Component drawing and placement - Timer management (hourglass) - Component reservation (for advanced levels) - Assembly completion
See Also:

    private Map<Integer,Player>
    finishedPlayers
    Map of players who have finished assembly with their starting position
    private Map<Integer,List<Integer>>
    validCoordinates
    Valid coordinates for component placement based on game level, key is the row i, values are the corresponding valid columns

    Fields inherited from class Controller.State

    context

  • Field Details

    • finishedPlayers

      private Map<Integer,Player> finishedPlayers
      Map of players who have finished assembly with their starting position

    • validCoordinates

      private Map<Integer,List<Integer>> validCoordinates
      Valid coordinates for component placement based on game level, key is the row i, values are the corresponding valid columns

  • Constructor Details

    • BuildingState

      public BuildingState(Controller controller)
      Constructor that initializes the building state. Sets up valid coordinates based on match level (Trial or Level 2).
      Parameters:
      controller - The game controller
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the match level is invalid

  • Method Details

    • unbookDeck

      private void unbookDeck(String player)
      Unbooks the deck for a player if they have booked one. This is called when a player draws a component or reserves a component.
      Parameters:
      player - The player whose deck booking is to be removed

    • getComponent

      public void getComponent(String name, int index) throws InvalidCommand, InvalidParameters
      Allows a player to draw a component from the common pool. The component becomes the player's active component.
      Overrides:
      getComponent in class State
      Parameters:
      name - Player's name
      index - Index of the component in the pool
      Throws:
      InvalidCommand - If the player has already finished or timer has expired
      InvalidParameters - If the index is invalid or player doesn't exist

    • reserveComponent

      public void reserveComponent(String name) throws InvalidCommand, InvalidParameters
      Allows a player to reserve their active component. Only available in advanced levels (not Trial).
      Overrides:
      reserveComponent in class State
      Parameters:
      name - Player's name
      Throws:
      InvalidCommand - If it's a Trial deck, player has finished, or has already reserved 2 components
      InvalidParameters - If the player doesn't exist

    • placeComponent

      public void placeComponent(String name, ComponentOrigin origin, Coordinates coordinates, Direction orientation) throws InvalidCommand, InvalidParameters
      Places a component on the player's board. The component can come from hand or reserved components.
      Overrides:
      placeComponent in class State
      Parameters:
      name - Player's name
      origin - Component origin (hand or reserved)
      coordinates - Coordinates where to place the component
      orientation - Component orientation
      Throws:
      InvalidCommand - If player has finished or has no component in the selected origin
      InvalidParameters - If coordinates are invalid or already occupied

    • lookDeck

      public void lookDeck(String name, int index) throws InvalidCommand, InvalidParameters
      Allows viewing one of the predictable adventure card decks. Only available in advanced levels.
      Overrides:
      lookDeck in class State
      Parameters:
      name - Player's name
      index - Index of the deck to view (1-3)
      Throws:
      InvalidCommand - If it's a Trial deck or player hasn't placed components yet
      InvalidParameters - If the index is invalid

    • flipHourGlass

      public void flipHourGlass(String name) throws InvalidCommand, InvalidParameters
      Flips the hourglass to proceed to the next phase. Only players who have finished can flip the last hourglass.
      Overrides:
      flipHourGlass in class State
      Parameters:
      name - Player's name
      Throws:
      InvalidCommand - If it's a Trial deck, hourglass isn't finished, or player cannot flip it (e.g. hasn't finished, and want to flip the last hourglass)
      InvalidParameters - If the player doesn't exist

    • preBuiltShip

      public void preBuiltShip(String name, int index) throws InvalidCommand, InvalidParameters
      Allows a player to choose a pre-built ship from the available options. The ship is set as the player's ship board.
      Overrides:
      preBuiltShip in class State
      Parameters:
      name - Player's name
      index - Index of the pre-built ship (0-2)
      Throws:
      InvalidCommand - If player has already finished or ship is invalid (Trial)
      InvalidParameters - If the index is invalid or player doesn't exist

    • finishBuilding

      public void finishBuilding(String name, int position) throws InvalidCommand, InvalidParameters
      Finishes assembly for a player and places them on the starting grid. If there are components reserved not placed, they are converted into junk (penalty).
      Overrides:
      finishBuilding in class State
      Parameters:
      name - Player's name
      position - Desired starting position (1-4)
      Throws:
      InvalidCommand - If player has already finished or ship is invalid (Trial)
      InvalidParameters - If position is already occupied

    • deleteComponent

      public void deleteComponent(String name, Coordinates coordinates) throws InvalidCommand, InvalidParameters
      Deletes a component from the player's ship board. Only available in Trial games.
      Overrides:
      deleteComponent in class State
      Parameters:
      name - Player's name
      coordinates - Coordinates of the component to delete
      Throws:
      InvalidCommand - If it's not a Trial game
      InvalidParameters - If the player doesn't exist or coordinates are invalid

    • getAvailableCommands

      public List<String> getAvailableCommands()
      Returns the list of available commands in this state. This includes commands for building, reserving, and placing components, as well as managing the hourglass and viewing decks. * @return List of available commands
      Specified by:
      getAvailableCommands in class State
